name: Putumayo (Colombia)

colors.symbolism: the lush Amazonian rainforest that covers most of the department
the peace, purity and rivers of the region
the petroleum wealth that drives the local economy

description: Three equal horizontal stripes, green, white and black from top to bottom.
history: Putumayo became a department of Colombia in 1991; its tricolour flag combines the green of the Amazon, the white of its rivers, and the black of its oil reserves.
